name: Time_Pods

sections.intro: TheTime Podis a special item inInto The Breachthat can randomly drop during missions.
sections.Description:
During eachMission, there is a chance for a pod to appear from the future, dropping it onto a random tile. This can happen in any Missionexceptfor ones with theProtect the Corporate Towerobjective.
A new objective is added when this event happens:Protect the Time Pod. The 1st and 2nd islands in a timeline will always have one Time Pod. The 3rd and 4th island will always have two Time Pods. Note that this number also includes the appearance of aStrange Pod, which has a 15% chance of replacing a Time Pod in the 2nd, 3rd or 4th island.
Protecting or rescuing the Pod always grants aReactor Core. Time Pods may also contain aPilot, aWeapon, or a piece ofPassiveequipment.
If the Vek end on the tile where the Pod is, or if it takes damage from a Mech or a Vek attack, the Pod will be destroyed. Projectile weapons can pass safely over the pod, but not piercing/beam weapons. A Pod is still destroyed if the Vek who steps on it has Flying. If the Pod remains untouched until the end of the mission, it is recovered as well.
sections.Trivia:
- The Pod will typically drop on the half of the map closest to where the Vek spawn.
  - Rescuing the Pod counts towards clearing an Island perfectly - if one is destroyed, it counts as a failed objective.
  - The Pod cannot be frozen.
  - Although tides do not do damage directly, they will still destroy the Pod. Tiles converted to Chasms will likewise destroy it. The same is not true of Ice Storms.
  - Smoking the tile will not destroy the time pod.
  - Setting fire to the tile in any way will destroy the time pod even if no actual damage is dealt.
  - Lighting the pod's tile on fire will destroy the pod, even if no actual damage was done to the tile.
  - Cracking the tile with theDrill Mechwill destroy the pod.
  - When a unit (excludingMechs) withacidinflicted is killed and pushed onto the pod, theA.C.I.D. Poolleft behind by the unit will destroy the pod.
  - Vek do not destroy the pod if they run over it, only if they finish their turn on the pod's square or they die on it.
  - Vek will not actively destroy the pod but sometimes AOE damages might happen to cover the tile. Vek sometimes walk onto pods even if it is rare. It can cause you to not be able to 100% an island.
  - Allied Units, such as theRenfield Bomb, can collect a pod if they're pushed or walk onto it.
  - An attacking beetle Vek will not destroy a pod if its attack passes over the pod.
  - If the pod is on a tile that is submerged by a brokenOld Earth Dam, the pod will be destroyed.

full_text:
The
Time Pod
is a special item in
Into The Breach
that can randomly drop during missions.
Pod
Description
[
]
During each
Mission
, there is a chance for a pod to appear from the future, dropping it onto a random tile. This can happen in any Mission
except
for ones with the
Protect the Corporate Tower
objective.
A new objective is added when this event happens:
Protect the Time Pod
. The 1st and 2nd islands in a timeline will always have one Time Pod. The 3rd and 4th island will always have two Time Pods. Note that this number also includes the appearance of a
Strange Pod
, which has a 15% chance of replacing a Time Pod in the 2nd, 3rd or 4th island.
Protecting or rescuing the Pod always grants a
Reactor Core
. Time Pods may also contain a
Pilot
, a
Weapon
, or a piece of
Passive
equipment.
If the Vek end on the tile where the Pod is, or if it takes damage from a Mech or a Vek attack, the Pod will be destroyed. Projectile weapons can pass safely over the pod, but not piercing/beam weapons. A Pod is still destroyed if the Vek who steps on it has Flying. If the Pod remains untouched until the end of the mission, it is recovered as well.
Trivia
[
]
The Pod will typically drop on the half of the map closest to where the Vek spawn.
Rescuing the Pod counts towards clearing an Island perfectly - if one is destroyed, it counts as a failed objective.
The Pod cannot be frozen.
Although tides do not do damage directly, they will still destroy the Pod. Tiles converted to Chasms will likewise destroy it. The same is not true of Ice Storms.
Smoking the tile will not destroy the time pod.
Setting fire to the tile in any way will destroy the time pod even if no actual damage is dealt.
Lighting the pod's tile on fire will destroy the pod, even if no actual damage was done to the tile.
Cracking the tile with the
Drill Mech
will destroy the pod.
When a unit (excluding
Mechs
) with
acid
inflicted is killed and pushed onto the pod, the
A.C.I.D. Pool
left behind by the unit will destroy the pod.
Vek do not destroy the pod if they run over it, only if they finish their turn on the pod's square or they die on it.
Vek will not actively destroy the pod but sometimes AOE damages might happen to cover the tile. Vek sometimes walk onto pods even if it is rare. It can cause you to not be able to 100% an island.
Allied Units
, such as the
Renfield Bomb
, can collect a pod if they're pushed or walk onto it.
An attacking beetle Vek will not destroy a pod if its attack passes over the pod.
If the pod is on a tile that is submerged by a broken
Old Earth Dam
, the pod will be destroyed.
